description: Remove all Telerik references from the Ralph sandbox — revert PR #43 code changes, remove network allow-host, clean up docs and task files

userStories.title: Revert New-NuGetConfig to pre-Telerik state
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the New-NuGetConfig function restored to its original signature (before PR #43) so there is no Telerik-related code in the NuGet config generation.
acceptanceCriteria: New-NuGetConfig signature is ($OutputPath, $GitHubPat) — no $TelerikPassword parameter
All Telerik conditional XML generation is removed ($telerikSource, $telerikCredentials, $telerikMapping variables)
The $safePat XML-escaping of $GitHubPat is kept — this is a useful security improvement
The generated nuget.config template has no Telerik interpolations
The nuget.config output contains only nuget.org and GitHub-FreemarketFX sources
Build passes
userStories.notes: Keep [System.Security.SecurityElement]::Escape($GitHubPat) — it was added in PR #43 but is a good security fix. Only revert the Telerik-specific additions.

userStories.title: Remove Telerik credential resolution block
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Telerik token environment variable check and interactive prompt removed from the main script flow.
acceptanceCriteria: The $TelerikPassword variable assignment block (~lines 376-383) is completely removed
The $env:TELERIK_TOKEN check is removed
The Read-Host prompt for Telerik NuGet token is removed
The -TelerikPassword $TelerikPassword argument is removed from the New-NuGetConfig call
Build passes
userStories.notes: The New-NuGetConfig call should become: New-NuGetConfig -OutputPath $nugetStaged -GitHubPat $GitHubPat

userStories.title: Remove Telerik from network allow-list and comments
userStories.description: As a developer, I want nuget.telerik.com removed from the sandbox network policy since no traffic should go to Telerik.
acceptanceCriteria: --allow-host "nuget.telerik.com" is removed from the network proxy command
The bypass-host comment referencing nuget.telerik.com is removed from the escape-hatch comments
The 'Common bypass candidates' list no longer mentions nuget.telerik.com
Build passes
userStories.notes: Lines ~452-456 have comments mentioning nuget.telerik.com as a bypass candidate. Line ~473 has the actual --allow-host.

userStories.title: Clean up Telerik references in documentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Telerik references removed from README.md and Dockerfile comments so documentation reflects current state.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph-sandbox/README.md: nuget.telerik.com removed from the network allow-list docs
ralph-sandbox/README.md: nuget.telerik.com bypass-host example removed or replaced with another example
ralph-sandbox/docker/Dockerfile.sandbox: comment updated from 'NuGet feeds (GitHub Packages, Telerik, etc.)' to 'NuGet feeds (GitHub Packages)'
Build passes
userStories.notes: README line ~74 lists nuget.telerik.com in the allow-list. Line ~106 shows it as a bypass-host example.

userStories.title: Clean up Telerik-related task files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Telerik-related PRD files archived or deleted so the tasks directory reflects current work.
acceptanceCriteria: tasks/prd-telerik-nuget-credentials.md is deleted
tasks/prd-sandbox-network-policies.md: Telerik-specific references removed (NuGet Telerik line, Telerik in test criteria)
tasks/archive/add-telerik-auth/ is left untouched (historical record)
Build passes
userStories.notes: prd-sandbox-network-policies.md line ~25 mentions 'NuGet (Telerik): nuget.telerik.com' and line ~81 mentions Telerik in NuGet restore test criteria.
